threats.title: mintYieldFee Missing Access Control Allows Anyone to Steal Accrued Yield Fees
threats.hypothesis_description: The Vault.mintYieldFee function is external with NO access control and ignores the _yieldFeeRecipient state variable. Any caller can mint accrued yield fee shares (_yieldFeeTotalSupply) to any arbitrary recipient address. This enables direct theft of all accumulated yield fees.
threats.investigation_rationale: This is a clear access control gap: the function should be restricted to the _yieldFeeRecipient or the owner, but neither is checked. The _yieldFeeRecipient address is completely ignored in the minting logic.

proof_of_signal.pattern: external function with no access modifier; _yieldFeeRecipient state var is never referenced in mintYieldFee; any address can be _recipient

preconditions: _yieldFeeTotalSupply > 0 (yield fees have been accumulated via liquidation)
_yieldFeePercentage > 0 (yield fee is active)
Vault is collateralized (_currentExchangeRate >= _assetUnit)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: mintYieldFee takes arbitrary _recipient, checks only _shares <= _yieldFeeTotalSupply and collateralization
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker calls mintYieldFee(_yieldFeeTotalSupply, attackerAddress) to mint all accrued fee shares to themselves
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker steals entire _yieldFeeTotalSupply worth of vault shares, redeemable for underlying assets

economic_questions: What is the expected _yieldFeeTotalSupply accumulation rate for a given vault?
Can the attacker front-run the legitimate yield fee recipient's mint call?
validation_requirements: Confirm no hidden access control inherited from parent contracts
Calculate expected yield fee accumulation per draw period

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: All accrued yield fees (depends on vault TVL and fee percentage)

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Immediately after any liquidation that increases _yieldFeeTotalSupply
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Reduces vault yield for all depositors; yield fee recipient receives nothing

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Yield fee recipient loses all accrued fees; vault depositors indirectly affected by dilution
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All accrued _yieldFeeTotalSupply across all vaults with active yield fees

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify no access control on mintYieldFee; confirm _yieldFeeRecipient is never checked

threats.title: Sponsor Function Griefing Forces Victim Delegation to Sponsorship Address, Revoking Prize Eligibility
threats.hypothesis_description: The Vault.sponsor function calls _twabController.sponsor(_receiver), which overwrites the receiver's delegation to SPONSORSHIP_ADDRESS for the calling vault, regardless of the receiver's wishes. An attacker can call sponsor(1, victimAddress) to deposit 1 wei for the victim while forcibly revoking their prize eligibility by delegating their entire balance to the sponsorship address.
threats.investigation_rationale: The sponsor function unconditionally overrides the receiver's delegation. Since any address can be the receiver, this is a permissionless griefing vector that removes victims from the prize-winning pool.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _twabController.sponsor(_receiver) called unconditionally after deposit, overwriting receiver's delegation without consent

preconditions: Vault is collateralized (so deposit succeeds)
Victim has non-zero balance in the vault
Victim is not already delegating to SPONSORSHIP_ADDRESS

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: sponsor(1 wei, victim) forces victim.delegation = SPONSORSHIP_ADDRESS
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker calls sponsor with minimal deposit, victim loses delegateBalance → winning zone becomes 0
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: If attacker is also a depositor, removing a victim from the prize pool increases attacker's relative winning odds

economic_questions: Can the victim undo the delegation change quickly enough to not miss prize eligibility?
How many users can be griefed in a single transaction via multicall?
validation_requirements: Confirm TwabController.delegate can be used by victim to restore delegation
Calculate TWAB impact window of temporary delegation change

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: ~0.01 ETH for many victims on L2
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Indirect: increased prize odds proportional to removed delegateBalance
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Variable, depends on victim balance relative to total supply
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Instantaneous per victim; victim must actively re-delegate

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Before draw closure to maximize TWAB impact
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Targeted griefing of specific high-value depositors

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Any vault depositor targeted by the attacker
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Complete loss of prize eligibility until victim re-delegates
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Entire expected prize value for the affected draw period

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: TWAB period length, delegation change impact on TWAB calculation
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Test that sponsor(1, victim) actually changes delegation and impacts winning zone

threats.title: Single Canary Tier Claim Bypasses Expansion Threshold, Forcing Premature Tier Expansion
threats.hypothesis_description: In _computeNextNumberOfTiers, _nextNumberOfTiers is initially set to largestTierClaimed + 2. When the canary tier (numberOfTiers - 1) is claimed, this sets _nextNumberOfTiers to numberOfTiers + 1 BEFORE the threshold check. The threshold check can only further increase (not decrease) this value, so tier expansion occurs regardless of whether the claimExpansionThreshold conditions are met.
threats.investigation_rationale: The logic error causes the initial _nextNumberOfTiers value to already be the expanded count when a canary prize is claimed, making the expansion threshold check redundant. This can lead to runaway tier expansion that dilutes prize liquidity across too many tiers.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _nextNumberOfTiers = largestTierClaimed + 2 sets expansion value before threshold gate; if-block condition _nextNumberOfTiers >= _numTiers is already true when canary is claimed

preconditions: At least one canary tier prize is claimed in the current draw
lastClosedDrawId != 0 (not the first draw)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: largestTierClaimed = numberOfTiers - 1 (canary) → _nextNumberOfTiers = numberOfTiers + 1 regardless of canaryClaimCount or claimCount thresholds
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Claim a single canary prize (high odds tier) to force tier expansion, diluting prize liquidity
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker forces premature expansion to create more low-tier prizes with higher odds, at the expense of higher-tier prize sizes

economic_questions: How quickly can tiers expand from minimum (3) to maximum (15)?
What is the quantitative impact on prize sizes when a tier is added prematurely?
Can an attacker profit by claiming more frequent but smaller prizes in expanded tiers?
validation_requirements: Trace _computeNextNumberOfTiers logic with concrete values
Calculate prize size dilution per premature tier expansion
Determine if expansion can be reversed (contraction logic)

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Cost of claiming one canary prize (gas only, claim fee goes to claimer)
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Indirect: manipulated prize distribution in subsequent draws
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Variable, depends on vault's contribution and attacker's TWAB
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Persistent effect across subsequent draws until contraction

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Every draw where canary prizes are claimable (high odds = frequent)
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Systematic prize dilution affecting all depositors

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Reduced prize sizes across all tiers due to liquidity dilution
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Entire prize pool liquidity distribution is distorted

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Step through _computeNextNumberOfTiers with canary claimed to confirm bypass

threats.title: Reverting Winner Hooks Cause Batch Claim DoS and Gas Griefing of Claimers
threats.hypothesis_description: Winners can set arbitrary hook implementations via setHooks. A malicious hook that reverts in beforeClaimPrize or afterClaimPrize causes the entire batch claim transaction (processing multiple winners) in Vault.claimPrizes to revert. This griefs claimers who waste gas and prevents other legitimate winners in the same batch from receiving their prizes.
threats.investigation_rationale: The Vault._claimPrize function makes external calls to user-controlled hook contracts without try/catch protection. A single reverting hook poisons an entire batch of claims.

proof_of_signal.pattern: hooks.implementation.beforeClaimPrize and afterClaimPrize called without try/catch; revert propagates to batch claimPrizes caller

preconditions: Winner sets a hook with useBeforeClaimPrize=true or useAfterClaimPrize=true
Hook implementation reverts on call
Claimer includes the malicious winner in a batch claim

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Single reverting hook in a batch of N claims wastes all gas for N claims
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker sets reverting hook, forces claimers to exclude them from batches (trial and error, wasting gas)
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Griefing attack: attacker may benefit from delayed claims of other winners (reduced canary/claim counts affecting tier expansion)

economic_questions: How much gas does a failed batch claim cost?
Can the attacker profit from delayed claims by manipulating tier expansion/contraction?
validation_requirements: Confirm no try/catch on hook calls
Estimate gas cost per failed batch

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Gas to deploy reverting hook contract + gas to call setHooks
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Indirect: no direct profit; griefing cost imposed on claimers
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Persistent until claimer identifies and excludes the malicious winner

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Minimal (just need to be a depositor with a winning position)
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Delays prize claiming for all batched winners; claimers lose gas

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Claimers (gas costs) and co-batched winners (delayed prizes)
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Gas cost per failed batch (~200k-500k gas wasted)
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Claimer rewards and co-winners' prize timeliness

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Typical batch sizes, gas costs per claim
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Test batch claim failure with reverting hook

threats.title: Vault _deposit Skips Token Transfer When Vault Holds Idle Assets, Enabling Free Share Minting
threats.hypothesis_description: In Vault._deposit, when the vault's underlying asset balance (_vaultAssets) is >= the deposit amount (_assets), NO tokens are transferred from the caller. The vault uses its existing idle balance to deposit into the yield vault, and mints shares to the receiver for free. This allows theft of any underlying assets sitting in the vault contract.
threats.investigation_rationale: The _deposit function has an explicit code path where _assets <= _vaultAssets results in no safeTransferFrom call, yet full share minting occurs. This is a clear code defect where the depositor receives shares without payment.

proof_of_signal.pattern: if (_assets > _vaultAssets) { ... transfer ... } — else branch does no transfer but still calls _yieldVault.deposit and _mint

preconditions: Vault holds non-zero balance of underlying asset (via direct transfer, accidental send, or liquidation flow edge case)
Vault is collateralized

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker monitors vault's asset balance; when > 0, calls deposit(vaultBalance, attackerAddress) to receive free shares
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Profit = convertToAssets(shares_received) - 0 (no payment) = value of idle vault assets

economic_questions: Under what conditions do underlying assets accumulate in the vault?
Does the liquidation flow's unit mismatch (ECON-H006) reliably create idle assets?
Can an attacker atomically send assets + deposit to exploit this?
validation_requirements: Confirm the no-transfer code path with concrete test
Map all code paths that leave assets in the vault
Check if exchange rate update in _mint affects profitability

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: 0 if vault already holds idle assets; otherwise, the attacker donates and deposits to get shares (net zero for attacker unless another user donated)
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to the full amount of idle assets in the vault
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: 100% ROI on idle asset amount (no capital needed)

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Immediately after assets appear in the vault
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Dilutes existing shareholders proportionally to stolen amount

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Whoever left assets in the vault (donation/accident); indirectly all existing shareholders via dilution
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Proportional to idle asset amount relative to total vault assets
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All idle underlying assets in any vault

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Typical vault asset balance, frequency of idle assets

threats.title: Vault.liquidate Unit Mismatch Comparing Shares to Assets Prevents Idle Asset Sweep
threats.hypothesis_description: In Vault.liquidate, the condition `_amountOut >= _vaultAssets` compares _amountOut (denominated in vault shares) with _vaultAssets (denominated in underlying assets). When the exchange rate is not 1:1, this comparison is incorrect: a smaller number of shares may represent a larger economic value than the asset amount. This causes the yield vault deposit sweep to not trigger, leaving underlying assets stranded in the vault.
threats.investigation_rationale: This is a clear unit mismatch bug. It enables assets to accumulate in the vault, directly feeding the _deposit free share vulnerability (ECON-H005).

proof_of_signal.pattern: _amountOut (shares) >= _vaultAssets (underlying assets) — incompatible unit comparison; should convert _amountOut to assets first

preconditions: Exchange rate > 1 (each share is worth more than 1 asset unit)
Vault holds non-zero underlying asset balance
Liquidation occurs with _amountOut in shares less than _vaultAssets in tokens

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Exchange rate drift causes liquidation sweeps to fail, accumulating idle assets exploitable via ECON-H005
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Stranded assets become claimable via the free deposit bug

economic_questions: How quickly does the exchange rate diverge from 1:1 in production?
What is the typical _vaultAssets balance during liquidation?
validation_requirements: Test liquidation with exchange rate > 1 and verify assets are stranded
Quantify asset accumulation rate over multiple liquidation cycles

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Depends on liquidation frequency and exchange rate drift

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Vault shareholders (idle assets are not generating yield in yield vault)
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Reduced yield + potential theft via ECON-H005
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All underlying assets that fail to sweep

threats.title: Vault Portion Calculation Includes Open Draw Contributions Enabling Manipulation of Prize Odds
threats.hypothesis_description: In _computeVaultTierDetails, the vault portion is calculated using endDrawId = _lastClosedDrawId + 1 (the open draw). This includes smoothed contributions made to the current open draw in the vault's portion for CLOSED draw claims. An attacker can contribute to the open draw immediately after closeDraw to inflate their vault's portion before prizes are claimed.
threats.investigation_rationale: The endDrawId off-by-one means the vault portion for prize determination includes contributions from a draw period that hasn't been distributed yet, creating a manipulation window.

proof_of_signal.pattern: endDrawId = _lastClosedDrawId + 1 includes open draw contributions in vault portion used for closed draw prize determination

preconditions: Attacker controls a vault or can contribute on behalf of a vault
Smoothing parameter allows meaningful contribution from open draw
Prize claims haven't been executed yet for the closed draw

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: vaultPortion includes getDisbursedBetween(startDrawId, _lastClosedDrawId + 1) — the +1 includes open draw
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Contribute large amount to open draw before claims → inflate vault portion → increase winning odds
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Profit = increased prize winnings - cost of contribution

economic_questions: How much does smoothing attenuate open draw contributions?
What capital is needed to meaningfully shift the vault portion?
validation_requirements: Calculate vault portion with and without open draw contribution
Determine smoothing factor impact on single-draw contribution

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Significant POOL tokens (depends on existing total contributions)
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: Gas + opportunity cost of POOL tokens
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Increased share of prizes proportional to vault portion increase
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Single block window after closeDraw, before claims

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Immediately after closeDraw and before prize claims

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Reduced winning probability proportional to vault portion dilution
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Portion of prize pool that gets redistributed to manipulator

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Smoothing value, typical contribution sizes, total contributions per draw
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Quantify open draw contribution impact on vault portion

threats.title: yieldFeePercentage at FEE_PRECISION Causes Division by Zero in Liquidation, Permanently Bricking Yield Capture
threats.hypothesis_description: The _setYieldFeePercentage function allows yieldFeePercentage to be set to exactly FEE_PRECISION (1e9, i.e. 100%) because it uses > instead of >=. In Vault.liquidate, the calculation (FEE_PRECISION - _yieldFeePercentage) becomes 0, causing a division by zero that permanently bricks the liquidation mechanism.
threats.investigation_rationale: This is a clear off-by-one in validation. The boundary condition allows a value that makes a critical mathematical operation revert. Even if unintentional, it permanently disables prize funding.

proof_of_signal.pattern: if (yieldFeePercentage_ > FEE_PRECISION) revert — allows equality (1e9); liquidate line 391: (FEE_PRECISION - _yieldFeePercentage) = 0 → division by zero

preconditions: Vault owner sets yieldFeePercentage to exactly FEE_PRECISION (1e9)
_yieldFeePercentage != 0 check passes in liquidate

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Owner (malicious or mistaken) sets fee to 100%, causing all liquidation calls to revert
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit; denial of service prevents yield→prize conversion indefinitely

economic_questions: Is there a recovery mechanism if the fee is set to 100%?
Can the owner reduce the fee back to a safe value?
validation_requirements: Confirm _setYieldFeePercentage allows equality with FEE_PRECISION
Confirm liquidate reverts with division by zero at that value
Verify owner can reset the fee

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: No direct profit; blocks yield capture

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Complete halt of vault yield→prize conversion

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All vault depositors (no prizes generated from yield)
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Complete loss of prize opportunity from vault yield
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All future yield from the affected vault

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: FEE_PRECISION value, owner's ability to reset
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Test setYieldFeePercentage(1e9) and subsequent liquidate call

threats.title: setLiquidationPair Grants Unnecessary Max Asset Approval to LP, Enabling Full Vault Drain
threats.hypothesis_description: Vault.setLiquidationPair approves the new LiquidationPair for type(uint256).max of the UNDERLYING ASSET (e.g., USDC). The liquidation flow only involves prize tokens and vault shares — the LP should never need to pull underlying assets. A compromised or malicious LP contract can call asset.transferFrom(vault, attacker, balance) to drain all underlying assets held by the vault.
threats.investigation_rationale: This approval is unnecessary for the liquidation mechanism and creates an unrequired attack surface. The LP only needs to send prize tokens to the PrizePool and call liquidate — it never interacts with the underlying asset.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _asset.safeApprove(address(liquidationPair_), type(uint256).max) — approves underlying asset to LP which should only handle prize tokens

preconditions: Owner sets a compromised or malicious LiquidationPair address
Vault holds underlying assets (normally minimal, but possible during deposit flows)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: LP gets max approval on underlying asset; can call asset.transferFrom(vault, attacker, vaultBalance)
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Malicious LP or LP with vulnerability extracts all underlying assets from vault
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Profit = all underlying assets in vault at time of exploitation

economic_questions: How much underlying asset does the vault typically hold?
Can the LP interact with the approval during normal operations?
validation_requirements: Confirm the LP approval is on the underlying asset, not just on prize tokens
Verify the LP never needs underlying asset access in the normal flow

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to total underlying assets in the vault (typically minimal)

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When vault has assets in transit (during deposit flow)
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Total loss of vault underlying assets

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Proportional to their share of total vault deposits
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All underlying assets accessible via the approval

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Typical vault underlying balance, LP interaction pattern
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify approval grants access to underlying asset (not vault shares)

threats.title: PrizePool.setDrawManager Unrestricted When drawManager Is Zero, Enabling Hostile Takeover of Draw Authority
threats.hypothesis_description: PrizePool.setDrawManager has no access control — it can be called by anyone when drawManager == address(0). If the PrizePool is deployed with drawManager=address(0), any address can call setDrawManager to become the draw manager, gaining exclusive control over closeDraw (setting winning random numbers) and withdrawReserve (draining the reserve).
threats.investigation_rationale: The setDrawManager function is protected only by a check that drawManager is currently address(0). Combined with the constructor allowing address(0) for drawManager, this creates a window where any caller can seize permanent control of critical protocol functions.
deployment_assumptions: PrizePool is deployed with params.drawManager == address(0)
threats.config_evidence: PrizePool constructor allows address(0) for drawManager; setDrawManager only checks drawManager != address(0)

proof_of_signal.pattern: No auth check beyond 'if (drawManager != address(0)) revert'; anyone can call when drawManager == 0

preconditions: PrizePool deployed with drawManager == address(0)
setDrawManager has not yet been called by the legitimate deployer

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: setDrawManager(attacker) grants attacker closeDraw + withdrawReserve authority permanently
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker calls setDrawManager(attacker) → calls closeDraw with chosen random number → calls withdrawReserve to drain
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Profit = entire reserve + ability to manipulate all future draw outcomes

economic_questions: Is the PrizePool typically deployed with a non-zero drawManager?
What is the expected reserve balance at risk?
validation_requirements: Check if deployment scripts always set drawManager to non-zero
Verify no other mechanism can change drawManager after initial set

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Entire reserve + manipulation of all future prizes
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Single transaction for takeover; permanent control thereafter

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Immediately after PrizePool deployment with address(0) drawManager

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Complete loss of prizes and reserve
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Entire PrizePool reserve and future prize integrity

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Test setDrawManager with address(0) initial state

threats.title: PrizePool.contributePrizeTokens Allows Arbitrary Vault Attribution of Unaccounted Token Balance
threats.hypothesis_description: PrizePool.contributePrizeTokens has no access control and allows any caller to attribute unaccounted prize tokens (balanceOf - accountedBalance) to any vault address. If prize tokens arrive at the PrizePool through any mechanism (direct transfer, dust, rounding), an attacker can claim them as contributions for their own vault, inflating their vault's portion and winning odds.
threats.investigation_rationale: The function validates only that sufficient unaccounted tokens exist, but does not verify the caller is the vault or that the vault actually generated the contribution. The _prizeVault parameter is fully attacker-controlled.

proof_of_signal.pattern: No access control; _prizeVault parameter is arbitrary; deltaBalance check only verifies tokens exist, not their source

preconditions: Unaccounted prize tokens exist in the PrizePool (deltaBalance > 0)
Attacker deploys or designates a vault address they control

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: _deltaBalance = prizeToken.balanceOf(address(this)) - _accountedBalance(); anyone can attribute this to any vault
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Transfer prize tokens to PrizePool, then call contributePrizeTokens(attackerVault, amount) to credit attacker's vault
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Increased vault portion → increased winning probability → higher expected prize value

economic_questions: How often do unaccounted tokens appear in the PrizePool?
What is the cost-benefit of contributing to inflate vault portion?
How much does smoothing attenuate a one-time contribution?
validation_requirements: Verify contributePrizeTokens accepts arbitrary _prizeVault
Calculate vault portion change per unit of contribution

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Prize tokens to contribute (depends on desired vault portion increase)
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Increased prize winnings minus contribution cost
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Depends on smoothing effect and vault portion leverage

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Before draw closure to maximize portion for upcoming claims
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Dilutes other vaults' contributions and winning odds

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All other vaults' depositors (reduced winning probability)
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Portion of prize pool shifted to attacker's vault

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Smoothing parameter, typical total contributions per draw
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Quantify vault portion shift per contribution amount
